message.content:
In /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic, thoroughly explore how existing libm tests work:

1. Read test/test-math/ directory - all files including meson.build. How are individual math function tests structured?
2. Read test/meson.build to understand how math tests are registered and run.
3. Look at any existing accuracy/precision tests (test-fma.c, test-strtod.c, fenv.c, etc.) - how do they check correctness?
4. Check if there's any ULP (units in last place) testing infrastructure.
5. Look at test/complex-funcs.c to see how complex math tests work.
6. Check if there's a pattern for reference values / golden values in tests.
7. Look at how test/test-math/meson.build generates individual test cases.

I need to understand the testing patterns so we can design accuracy tests for all libm functions.
